phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 97.3 dBA, gradually settling to 74.3-79.3 dBA midway through session. Vocalization detected mid-session (confidence: 0.82), contributing to ambient baseline. Higher ambient noise detected; concentration requires active focus effort.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 66 bpm throughout session (baseline typical for calm, focused state). Data indicates user was in optimal physiological state for concentration.
phase_1.gps_motion: Geolocation data exhibits high initial uncertainty (2.7m) that improves to 2.2m as session progresses. Motion activity scattered throughout session with user frequently shifting position.

phase_2.correlations: The shift toward continued motion aligns with GPS signal stabilization. Audio channel demonstrates notable transitions.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session demonstrates strong focus-supportive conditions with low ambient noise (79.3 dB), frequent body repositioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Seek quieter environment immediately—current >75dB threshold severely impacts focus. Current 93-second duration too brief for meaningful concentration assessment; aim for 10+ min sessions.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (79.3dB), user-state stability (active), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (93s).
